* fix(copilot-cli): handle gh built-in promotion in 2.x+

gh 2.89.0 promotes 'gh copilot' to a built-in command. The previous
fix checked gh alias list, which doesn't catch built-ins.

Two-part fix:
1. Idempotency check now uses 'gh copilot --help' — works for
   built-ins, extensions, and aliases.
2. Wrap install attempt with set +e to capture the "matches built-in"
   error and exit gracefully instead of failing the whole setup.

Alias guard retained as defensive code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(copilot-cli): use 'gh copilot -- --help' for idempotency check

On gh 2.89.0+, 'gh copilot --help' exits 0 even before the Copilot
CLI binary has been downloaded — it shows gh's own wrapper help and
never touches the binary. This caused the idempotency check to always
short-circuit to 'already available' on a fresh system.

Fix: use 'gh copilot -- --help' instead. The '--' passes the flag
through to the actual Copilot CLI binary, so:
  - binary already present → exits 0 immediately (idempotent)
  - binary not yet downloaded → triggers proactive download, then exits 0
  - older gh without extension → exits non-zero → falls through to install

Also update the file-header comment to document this nuance.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(shell): merge stderr into stdout for ordered output in piped environments (#68) (#70)

Add \xec 2>&1\ immediately after \set -euo pipefail\ in both
setup.sh and scripts/linux/setup.sh. This merges stderr into stdout
so log_error() output appears in-sequence with all other log output
when running inside a Devcontainer or piped environment.

The two tool scripts in scripts/linux/tools/ have no >&2 calls
and inherit the merged file descriptor from the parent — no changes
needed there.

Fixes #68

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(devcontainer): strip CRLF from .sh files on container create (#69) (#71)

Add onCreateCommand to strip carriage returns from all shell scripts
before postCreateCommand runs. This guards against the case where a
Windows user's working tree still has CRLF line endings after the
PR #66 gitattributes fix, which updates git's index but NOT the
working tree.

  find . -name '*.sh' | xargs sed -i 's/\r//'

- Safe on Linux: no-op if files are already LF
- Defensive against future CRLF drift from any Windows checkout
- No-op in GitHub Codespaces (fresh clone is always LF)
- Runs BEFORE postCreateCommand so bash setup.sh never sees \r

Fixes #69

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(jetson): harden setup script against all failure modes

- Step 6: Add curl to system packages (needed for model download in step 9)
- Step 8: Create config.json if missing (no repo file AND no example)
  Wrap provisioning in set +e so failure doesn't abort entire setup
- Step 9: Use CFG_SUPA_URL/KEY to avoid env var collision; remove hard
  exit 1 for missing config — warn and continue instead
  Add 2>/dev/null to curl/stat to prevent set -e crash
- Step 10: Check trtexec exists before attempting conversion; wrap in
  set +e so conversion failure doesn't abort setup
- Step 12: Check /usr/share/applications exists before creating shortcut
  (headless Jetson may not have desktop directory)

All non-blocking failures now warn and continue instead of aborting.
Only 2 hard exits remain: non-ARM64 abort and missing Docker.

commit.message:
Upgrade HTTP endpoints to HTTPS in Azure App Service (#16060)

* Upgrade HTTP endpoints to HTTPS in Azure App Service

HTTP endpoints are now upgraded to HTTPS:443 by default in App Service,
matching the existing behavior in Azure Container Apps. App Service already
redirects HTTP to HTTPS at the platform level, so this ensures connection
strings reflect the actual serving scheme.

- Add PreserveHttpEndpoints property to AzureAppServiceEnvironmentResource
- Add WithHttpsUpgrade() extension method to opt out
- Upgrade HTTP->HTTPS in ProcessEndpoints with consolidated logging
- Add CanPreserveHttpSchemeUsingWithHttpsUpgrade test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Metaculus: use continuous_range quantile array for P(date < cutoff)

The v3 API returns possibilities.continuous_range: a 201-point array of ISO
date strings representing the inverse CDF (quantile function) of the community
forecast. Replaced broken aggregations-based approach with binary search +
interpolation on this array.

Don't guess at variants when TCGPlayer has no data. Sets without
price data (new/unreleased) show one entry per card number. Variant
expansion only happens when the API actually reports multiple price
types. We show what we know, not what we assume.

available for this set from TCGPlayer.' This honestly communicates
the limitation for new sets instead of silently showing incomplete
results or guessing at variants.

from actual TCGPlayer price type data.

Fix Response body consumed error: dedup via parsed JSON not Response (#39)

The request deduplication cached Promise<Response> and tried to
clone it for subsequent callers. But Response.clone() fails after
the body is consumed by the first caller's .json() call.

Fix: add scheduledFetchJson<T>() that caches the parsed JSON promise
instead. All callers share the same parsed result with no body
consumption issues. Updated all call sites in pokemonTcgApi.ts and

* fix: add unique constraint migration for project_amenities on cold start

ON CONFLICT (project_id, amenity) requires a UNIQUE constraint on that
column pair. The constraint was missing from production DB, causing
42P10 error and 500 on every POST /admin/properties call.

Migration adds the constraint idempotently on cold start.

Replace p.id = $1 with p.id::text = $1 in GET /projects/:id,
GET internal helper, PATCH /admin/properties/:id, and
DELETE /admin/properties/:id to fix 'operator does not exist:
text = uuid' error in PostgreSQL.

commit.message:
fix: make GHCR packages public after push so Railway can pull images

After each image build+push, call GitHub REST API to set container
package visibility to public. Railway cannot authenticate to private
GHCR packages and silently rolls back to the old container when the
image pull fails with 401.

Using GITHUB_TOKEN (packages: write permission already declared in
workflow) to PATCH /orgs/{owner}/packages/container/{pkg} with
visibility=public. The step is non-fatal — warns on failure but does
not block the deploy trigger.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
